policy: Root-level decisions cover scanned source roots that do not enter duplicate-name or duplicate-digest review queues but still need explicit retention, promotion, or review handling before source turnoff.

entries.rationale: Apollo codebase-navigator material has been synthesized into skills/local-repository-navigation as host-neutral guidance for git-excluded local navigation summaries. The Apollo provider-specific metadata remains external provenance rather than a canonical agent profile.
entries.nextAction: Use skills/local-repository-navigation through the repository-orientation plugin. Keep Apollo source files in place until a separate cleanup-ledger proposal and approval packet are reviewed.

entries.rationale: This root is installed marketplace and cache material from Claude/OpenAI/third-party plugin distributions. Treat it as external provenance, not canonical source, to avoid raw duplication, provider-name collisions, and accidental ownership of first-party plugin payloads.
entries.nextAction: Retain this root externally. Promote only renamed, rewritten, locally reviewed derivatives into this repository.

entries.rationale: The remaining singleton instruction is Examplar repository guidance. It should remain owned by Examplar unless a future review extracts provider-neutral guidance into this repository.
entries.nextAction: Retain Examplar instructions externally; promote only rewritten portable guidance if a future gap appears.

entries.rationale: The remaining singleton plugin belongs to Examplar's marketplace/source graph. This repository already contains synthesized primitive-authoring, governance, and orientation plugins rather than copying Examplar's plugin payload.
entries.nextAction: Retain the Examplar plugin externally; revisit only if this repository needs a deliberately renamed derivative.

entries.rationale: This root is an active installed skill runtime containing first-party, third-party, and local runtime copies. Covered entries already have canonical review decisions, while retained entries should stay with their owning runtime or provider distribution to avoid raw duplication and name collisions.
entries.nextAction: Retain this runtime root externally. Use this repository's canonical skills for new composition, and activate links into ~/.agents/skills only through the runtime-activation approval queue.

entries.rationale: This root is a historical backup store, not an active runtime surface. Its overlapping entries are already covered by source-review decisions, and remaining singleton backup entries should stay as provenance unless a future promotion explicitly rewrites them.
entries.nextAction: Retain this backup root as provenance. Do not add runtime replacement proposals for backup-only material unless a future audit promotes a canonical derivative.

entries.rationale: This root is a historical backup of older Codex skills. Covered entries already have canonical or retained-external review decisions, while the remaining singleton backup material should not be promoted or removed from this repository without a separate synthesis audit.
entries.nextAction: Retain this backup root as provenance. Use the current canonical skills and plugins for new work, and revisit individual backup skills only when a capability gap is identified.

entries.rationale: Kast agent-skill material is repo-specific and includes examples, references, command fixtures, and Kast maintenance context. Keep it owned by Kast; promote only rewritten generic lessons into this repository.
entries.nextAction: Retain the Kast root externally. Revisit individual entries only when designing a Kast-specific plugin family.

entries.rationale: The only discovered primitive is a Kast-specific GitHub agent that requires Kast runtime behavior, Kast project memory, and project-local AST workflow conventions. It should remain owned by the Kast repository rather than becoming a general-purpose personal toolbox primitive in this pass.
entries.nextAction: Retain the source root as an external Kast owner. Revisit only if a dedicated Kast plugin family is designed in this repository.

entries.rationale: Kast GitHub hooks are repo runtime infrastructure. This repository has already extracted generic hook lessons into canonical hook primitives; the remaining hook files should stay owned by Kast unless a future Kast-specific plugin is designed.
entries.nextAction: Retain these hooks in Kast and do not replace them from this repository without a dedicated Kast runtime activation plan.
